Scenario.title: The 2016 Ukraine Electric Power Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple The attack begins with targeted spearphishing emails and culminates in the disruption of communication services through manipulation of industrial control systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shared DB or directory, Authentication issues

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-2883 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office memory corruption vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 511 - Infiltration of Software Development Environment
stage.description: The attackers sent targeted spearphishing emails to employees in the communications sector, with malicious attachments disguised as legitimate documents. These attachments exploited a vulnerability in Microsoft Office to gain initial access to the victim's computer.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-24816 [CWE-94]: Path Traversal vulnerability in Apache HTTP Server
stage.description: Upon opening the attachment, it downloaded and executed Industroyer malware onto the victim's computer. The malware exploited a path traversal vulnerability in the Apache HTTP Server to remotely copy files, including itself, onto the victim's system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: To avoid detection, Industroyer disabled security software and tools running on the infected systems, ensuring it could operate undetected while causing maximum impact.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675 - Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The malware targeted and deleted critical files and data related to communication services and infrastructure, disrupting operations and causing significant damage.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Industroyer caused disruption by manipulating industrial control systems, leading to power outages and other service disruptions, effectively denying service to affected users and organizations.

Scenario.summary: The 2016 Ukraine Electric Power Attack was a sophisticated, expert-level operation targeting the communications sector. It began with a spearphishing campaign exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office, followed by the deployment of Industroyer malware via a path traversal vulnerability in the Apache HTTP Server. The malware then disabled security software, destroyed critical data, and manipulated industrial control systems to cause widespread disruption and denial of service.
